marketState: neutral-to-bullish — seasonally supportive but short-term overbought and slightly rich to fair value
summary: Seasonality is constructive: price is running +3.3pp above the median seasonal path, and the calendar is about to turn into September, historically the strongest month, with 56-63% historical odds of higher prices over the next 1-4 weeks. Weather demand is running +9% to +12.8% above the 5-year normal across both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows, though the demand index has cooled -32.2 over the last 24h, tempering near-term urgency. Front price ($3.245) is already 'SLIGHTLY OVER' modeled fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) at every horizon out to +21D, which caps immediate upside and raises pullback risk, especially with 5m/15m technicals deeply overbought (RSI 83.8/74.1, price above the upper Bollinger band) against a bearish weekly trend. Managed-money positioning is a contrarian tailwind — net short at only the 18th percentile of the 5-year range, a crowded-short setup vulnerable to squeezes — but storage sits +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf surprise, a structural supply overhang that argues against chasing rallies. Net: a seasonally bullish multi-week window offset by near-term technical exhaustion and a supply-side ceiling.

longTerm.entry: $3.15-$3.25 (scale in on dips toward current price/fair value)
longTerm.rationale: Price is running above the seasonal median (+3.3pp) heading into September, the historically strongest month, with 56-63% odds of higher prices over 1-4 weeks and a typical +2.3% 4-week move; weather demand is +9% to +12.8% above 5y normal across both windows; managed money is net short at just the 18th percentile of its 5y range, a crowded position vulnerable to short covering.
longTerm.invalidation: Weather forecasts flip below-normal/cooler, storage surplus vs 5y widens well beyond +175 Bcf with repeated bearish surprises, or managed money rapidly rebuilds net length, removing the short-squeeze setup.

shortTerm.rationale: 5m/15m technicals are sharply overbought (RSI 83.8/74.1) with price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h, front price is already 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value ($3.245 vs $2.929 fair), and the weekly trend reads bearish (RSI 45.4) — favors a near-term mean-reversion pullback before any resumption of the longer seasonal uptrend.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.36/upper Bollinger, materially hotter weather updates (demand_8_16D pctVs5y rising further), or a smaller-than-expected storage build would invalidate the fade and argue for covering.

Weather demand +9% to +12.8% above 5y normal (hot summer cooling load), though decelerating over the last 24h
Managed money net short at 18th percentile of 5y range — crowded short, squeeze risk
Price 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value across front/next contracts at all horizons — limits near-term upside
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with bearish +13 Bcf surprise — structural overhang capping rallies
risks: Seasonality reliability is LOW (51pp 10-90 spread) — wide uncertainty around the historical edge
